442:     fn anc(branch: &str, number: Option<u64>, url: Option<&str>) -> AncestorPr {

444:             branch: branch.to_string(),
445:             pr_number: number,
446:             pr_url: url.map(|s| s.to_string()),
447:         }

581:     #[test]
582:     fn parse_ez_body_no_markers() {
583:         let parsed = parse_ez_body("plain body text");
584:         assert!(!parsed.has_ez_markers);

590:     #[test]
591:     fn parse_ez_body_with_all_sections() {
592:         let body = format!(
593:             "My custom description\n\n{}\n{}\n\n{}\nPart of a stack.\n{}\n\n{}\nStack content\n{}\n\n{}\n{}",
594:             EZ_BEGIN, EZ_BELOW, SUMMARY_BEGIN, SUMMARY_END, STACK_BEGIN, STACK_END, EZ_ABOVE, EZ_END
595:         );
596:         let parsed = parse_ez_body(&body);
597:         assert!(parsed.has_ez_markers);
598:         assert_eq!(parsed.user_body, "My custom description");
599:         assert_eq!(parsed.summary.as_deref(), Some("Part of a stack."));
600:         assert!(parsed.has_summary_markers);
601:         assert_eq!(parsed.stack.as_deref(), Some("Stack content"));
602:         assert!(parsed.has_stack_markers);
603:     }

610:         );
611:         let parsed = parse_ez_body(&body);

678:     #[test]
679:     fn render_linear_tree() {
680:         let nodes = vec![StackNode {
681:             branch: "feat/a".to_string(),
682:             pr_number: Some(1),
683:             pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/1".to_string()),
684:             is_current: false,
685:             children: vec![StackNode {
686:                 branch: "feat/b".to_string(),
687:                 pr_number: Some(2),
688:                 pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/2".to_string()),
689:                 is_current: true,
690:                 children: vec![],
691:             }],
692:         }];
693:         let rendered = render_full_tree(&nodes);
694:         assert!(rendered.contains("this PR is 2 of 2"));
695:         assert!(rendered.contains("1. feat/a"));
696:         assert!(rendered.contains("2. **feat/b** \u{2190} you are here"));
697:     }

700:     fn render_branching_tree() {

702:             branch: "feat/a".to_string(),

704:             pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/1".to_string()),

706:             children: vec![

711:                     is_current: false,
712:                     children: vec![],

714:                 StackNode {
715:                     branch: "feat/c".to_string(),
716:                     pr_number: Some(3),
717:                     pr_url: Some("https://github.com/org/repo/pull/3".to_string()),
718:                     is_current: false,
719:                     children: vec![],
720:                 },
721:             ],
722:         }];

728:         assert!(rendered.contains("- feat/c"));

759:     // --- Contiguous PR chain tests ---
760: 
761:     #[test]
762:     fn contiguous_chain_linear_all_prs() {
763:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
764:         state.add_branch("a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
765:         state.add_branch("b", "a", "bbb", None, None);
766:         state.add_branch("c", "b", "ccc", None, None);
767:         state.get_branch_mut("a").unwrap().pr_number = Some(1);
768:         state.get_branch_mut("b").unwrap().pr_number = Some(2);
769:         state.get_branch_mut("c").unwrap().pr_number = Some(3);
770: 
771:         let chain = contiguous_pr_chain(&state, "b");
772:         assert!(chain.contains(&"a".to_string()));
773:         assert!(chain.contains(&"b".to_string()));
774:         assert!(chain.contains(&"c".to_string()));
775:     }
776: 
777:     #[test]
778:     fn contiguous_chain_gap_stops_ancestor() {
779:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
780:         state.add_branch("a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
781:         state.add_branch("b", "a", "bbb", None, None);
782:         state.add_branch("c", "b", "ccc", None, None);
783:         // a has no PR — gap
784:         state.get_branch_mut("b").unwrap().pr_number = Some(2);
785:         state.get_branch_mut("c").unwrap().pr_number = Some(3);
786: 
787:         let chain = contiguous_pr_chain(&state, "c");
788:         // Should not include a (no PR) but should include b and c.
789:         assert!(!chain.contains(&"a".to_string()));
790:         assert!(chain.contains(&"b".to_string()));
791:         assert!(chain.contains(&"c".to_string()));
792:     }
793: 
794:     #[test]
795:     fn contiguous_chain_gap_stops_children() {
796:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());
797:         state.add_branch("a", "main", "aaa", None, None);
798:         state.add_branch("b", "a", "bbb", None, None);
799:         state.add_branch("c", "b", "ccc", None, None);
800:         state.get_branch_mut("a").unwrap().pr_number = Some(1);
801:         // b has no PR — gap
802:         state.get_branch_mut("c").unwrap().pr_number = Some(3);
803: 
804:         let chain = contiguous_pr_chain(&state, "a");
805:         assert!(chain.contains(&"a".to_string()));
806:         // b has no PR, so c (past the gap) should NOT be included.
807:         assert!(!chain.contains(&"b".to_string()));
808:         assert!(!chain.contains(&"c".to_string()));
809:     }
810: }
